name: Steak with Honeyed Red Onions
recipeIngredient: 1 large Red Onion, thinly sliced and separated into rings
1 lb Beef Top Loin, cut into 1-inch thick pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium nonreactive mixing bowl, stir together red wine vinegar, honey, and thyme.
2. Add onion slices to the vinegar mixture. Let stand while preparing meat, stirring occasionally.
3. Trim fat from the beef; cut into serving-sized pieces. Sprinkle both sides of steak with cracked pepper, pressing it onto the surface of the meat.
4. In a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat, cook steaks for 10 minutes, turning once. Remove steaks to a plate and set aside, reserving drippings in the skillet.
5. Add the onion mixture to the drippings in the skillet. Cook over medium heat for 3 to 4 minutes or until onions are just crisp-tender, stirring occasionally.
6. Return steaks and any meat juices that accumulated on the plate back to the skillet. Reduce heat to medium-low.
7. Cook, uncovered, for an additional 3 to 4 minutes or until steak reaches desired doneness and liquid is slightly reduced, occasionally spooning the cooking liquid over the steaks.
8. To serve, transfer steaks to serving plates. Stir snipped parsley into onion mixture and spoon the onions over the steaks.
